title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed the lawsuit of Babson College student Any Lucia Lopez Belloza, deported to Honduras.
Lopez Belloza was mistakenly deported after being detained at Logan Airport despite a court order to keep her in the U.S.
Her appeal is pending; Lopez Belloza continues studying remotely from Honduras as legal team contests jurisdiction in Boston.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson praises Derrick White as a 'top-5 player' based on advanced analytics.
Derrick White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, 5.7 assists, 1.2 steals, and 1.5 blocks per game this season.
With Jayson Tatum missing 61 games, White stepped up as Boston's secondary scoring option behind Jaylen Brown.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scored 23 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-98 victory over the Cavaliers in Cleveland.
Jayson Tatum, returning from injury, scored 20 points and showed improvement but is not yet fully healthy.
The Celtics' defense excelled, holding the Cavaliers to 10 points in the second quarter with strong shot contests.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alec Pierce, Colts wide receiver, led NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025 seasons.
The Patriots have over $50 million in cap space but face multiple roster upgrade needs.
Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advises against a $30 million per year contract for Pierce.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown Officer Brittany Dougal, 29, died by suicide on March 4 after facing mental health challenges, police announced.
Dougal graduated from the Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy in December and began her role in North Kingstown shortly after.
Her family suggests donations to the North Kingstown Animal Shelter or Tunnel to Towers Foundation in memory of Officer Dougal.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jalen Nailor, a Vikings WR, is under consideration by the Patriots for his versatility and potential as a depth option.
Giants TE Daniel Bellinger is a potential target for the Patriots, offering size and run-blocking skills as a complementary piece.
EDGE player Joseph Ossai from the Bengals is highlighted for his pass-rushing potential and ability to bolster the Patriots' defense.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady may have influenced Maxx Crosby's trade away from the Patriots due to his role as a Raiders minority owner.
The Raiders traded Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall pick in the 2026 draft.
The Patriots might need to explore free agency to strengthen their edge rusher position, with names like Jaelan Phillips and Boye Mafe mentioned.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce expected to sign a contract over $27M annually, possibly staying with Indianapolis.
The Colts are negotiating with Pierce, while his desire to explore free agency remains strong.
Patriots showed interest in Pierce for wide receiver depth after releasing Stefon Diggs.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon, despite overcoming dyslexia, ADHD, and a rare vascular condition.
Flynn's connection to libraries began early, spending time at the Wayland Public Library where her mother worked as a librarian.
She is running in support of the Boston Public Library, emphasizing its role in literacy, education, and community accessibility.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, will run her 13th Boston Marathon on April 20, 2026.
Comander is running to support the Ellie Fund and honor her patient, Christina, facing advanced breast cancer.
The Ellie Fund aids breast cancer patients with services like transportation, meal preparation, and childcare reimbursement.
